news.headline: Pentagon Spokesman: Despite some incidents, ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah is holding.
Aprio's 2025 Technology Outlook details 6 key trends shaping tech sector investments, released by the 25th largest advisory firm.
news.summary: Atlanta, on December 2, 2024, Aprio, the 25th largest business advisory and accounting firm, unveiled its 2025 Technology Outlook, discussing six crucial trends set to shape tech sector investments in the upcoming year. The report emphasizes the significant influence of private sector advancements on technology.

news.headline: MicroStrategy's Michael Saylor compares Bitcoin to Manhattan, expects it to last 1,000 years, emphasizing its unique qualities.
news.summary: In an interview, Michael Saylor, executive chairman and co-founder of MicroStrategy (NASDAQ:MSTR), compared Bitcoin to "Manhattan in cyberspace," portraying it as a durable asset. Saylor accentuated Bitcoin's distinct features, such as its limited supply and global digital commodity status, underscoring its value as a store of wealth without relying on intermediaries. Describing Bitcoin as indestructible and immortal, Saylor noted its ability to transcend traditional financial and physical assets into the digital realm. He likened the influx of money into Bitcoin as a shift from the 20th-century financial landscape to the 21st century. MicroStrategy's incorporation of Bitcoin into its strategy has significantly influenced its stock performance. For more details, refer to the complete story on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Crypto analyst Michael van de Poppe predicts 70% rally for altcoin STX, suggests Bitcoin could reach $100K. Market shows shift from BTC to altcoins.
news.summary: A well-known cryptocurrency analyst, Michaël van de Poppe, has made a prediction on the market, suggesting a substantial rally for a top altcoin and Bitcoin potentially reaching a historic surge. This forecast arrives as the cryptocurrency landscape indicates a transition from Bitcoin dominance to an altcoin season. Van de Poppe is foreseeing a significant breakout for Stacks (STX), a prominent altcoin ranked in the top-50 cryptocurrencies. He envisions STX, the native token of the Bitcoin layer-2 project, experiencing a potential 70% rally as it approaches a crucial resistance level. The expert's analysis indicates a shift in the market dynamics with the focus moving towards altcoins. This prediction aligns with broader trends hinting at a potential rally in the overall cryptocurrency space.

news.headline: Dow rose 1.4% this week, Nasdaq Composite up over 6%, S&P 500 up 5%, Palantir up 275% YTD due to Elon Musk.
news.summary: The Dow rose by 1.4% in a week, with a November gain of 7.5%, while the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite increased by 1.1%, ending the month up over 5% and 6% respectively. President-elect Trump announced a 25% tariff on Mexican and Canadian imports and a 10% hike on Chinese goods. Despite concerns, the broader equity markets stayed strong. The Mexican peso and Canadian dollar fell to multi-year lows but stabilized later. Small-cap stocks surged to all-time highs. Benzinga highlighted the 275% YTD rally of Palantir Technologies Inc. attributed to Elon Musk according to Jim Cramer.

Stacey Yount appointed Chief Growth Officer at WCG to drive strategy and expansion. Dec. 2, 2024 PRNewswire.
news.summary: Stacey Yount has recently been appointed as the Chief Growth Officer at WCG, a prominent company based in Princeton, NJ. Effective December 2, 2024, this key position will involve overseeing the commercial aspects of the company's operations. WCG is known worldwide for its innovative solutions that significantly enhance and speed up clinical research processes.

news.headline: Changpeng Zhao open to pardon from Trump after pleading guilty to one charge & serving four months in prison.
news.summary: The ex-CEO of Binance has admitted to one felony charge and completed a four-month prison sentence. A potential presidential pardon might open up new prospects for him.

news.headline: Holston House Nashville launches Reindeer Games Pop-Up Bar to support CreatiVets, combining art and music for healing.
news.summary: Join Holston House Nashville in Nashville, Tennessee as they host their Annual Holiday Pop-Up, supporting veteran healing through art and music. The event, Reindeer Games Pop-Up Bar, is set to kick off on December 2, 2024. This festive celebration will combine various activities in a holiday-themed setting.

Global nephropathic cystinosis treatment market was valued at US$332.5 million in 2023 and expected to surpass US$744.3 million by 2034. Major players like Amgen Inc. and Recordati Rare Diseases are developing innovative drug therapies to improve patient outcomes.
news.summary: The nephropathic cystinosis treatment market was valued at US$332.5 million in 2023 and is expected to grow at a CAGR of 7.4% from 2024 to 2034, reaching over US$744.3 million by 2034. Key players in the market, such as Amgen Inc., Recordati Rare Diseases, and Chiesi Farmaceutici S.p.A., are focusing on developing cysteamine-based therapies to alleviate symptoms and improve the quality of life for patients. Nephropathic cystinosis, a rare genetic disorder causing cystine accumulation in cells, mainly affects the kidneys and other organs. The market growth is driven by advancements in drug therapies and increased awareness of the disease. Key players are actively involved in developing innovative treatments to manage symptoms and slow disease progression, with a growing focus on improving patient outcomes. Eloxx Pharmaceuticals Inc. is exploring RNA-based therapies for nephropathic cystinosis, contributing to the expansion of treatment options.

news.headline: Global running apparel market to reach USD 25,053.7 million by 2033, at a 5.1% CAGR; in 2024, valued at USD 16,012.1 million, increasing from USD 15,235.1 million in 2023. Trends include health awareness, tech advancements, female participation, e-commerce growth, and sustainability. Key players include ASICS and Lululemon.
news.summary: The global Running Apparel Market was valued at USD 15,235.1 Million in 2023 and is projected to reach USD 16,012.1 Million in 2024 and a potential value of around USD 25,053.7 Million by 2033, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of about 5.1% during the forecast period 2024 to 2033. Factors contributing to this growth include increasing health and fitness awareness, technological advancements in fabric and apparel design, rising popularity of outdoor and adventure activities, growth of e-commerce and online retail channels, increasing female participation in running, and sustainability trends. Key players like ASICS and Lululemon Athletica have made significant developments and expansions in the market. The report covers market size, growth factors, dynamics, and important segments by product type, gender, distribution channel, and region in North America, Europe, Asia Pacific, Middle East & Africa, and South & Central America.

news.headline: Guillermo Peschard Mijares appointed as EVP and President of Brink’s Latin America, effective Dec. 2, 2024. #Brinks #LatAm
news.summary: On December 2, 2024, in Richmond, Virginia, The Brink’s Company revealed that Guillermo Peschard Mijares is set to become the executive vice president and president of Brink’s Latin America (LATAM). This appointment makes him part of the executive leadership team, reporting directly to Mark Eubanks, the company’s president and CEO. Brink’s, recognized for its cash and valuables management, digital retail solutions (DRS), and ATM managed services (AMS), operates as a key player globally. Guillermo Peschard Mijares assumes his new role to oversee operations in Latin America.

news.headline: American Water Charitable Foundation gives $260,000 grant to National Urban League for workforce development initiatives.
news.summary: In Camden, New Jersey, the American Water Charitable Foundation, a 501(c)(3) organization linked to American Water, the biggest regulated water and wastewater utility firm in the U.S., revealed granting a $260,000 Corporate Strategic grant to the National Urban League. This funding will aid the Affiliate Movement, focusing on boosting workforce development initiatives to narrow the equality gap for African Americans and other historically marginalized communities nationwide.

Neurava adds Daniel Moore and Jed Kay to Board of Directors, bringing expertise in medical devices.
news.summary: Neurava, a leading medical device company specializing in epilepsy care, has revealed exciting news on December 2, 2024. They have appointed Daniel (Dan) Moore, former CEO of Cyberonics and former Chairman of the Board at LivaNova, as an independent member. This move is set to leverage Moore's expertise and experience in the field to advance the company's mission.

Aecon Utilities acquires Ainsworth's Power Construction business, expanding its electrical services and power systems capabilities.
news.summary: Aecon Utilities has increased its range of electrical services and power systems by acquiring Ainsworth’s Power Construction business. This acquisition will significantly enhance Aecon Utilities’ capabilities in these sectors. The move includes taking over Ainsworth’s Power Construction business, representing substantial growth opportunities for Aecon Utilities. The deal involves an undisclosed amount of investment, making it a strategic move for expanding the company's operations in the electrical services and power systems sectors. This acquisition is part of Aecon Utilities’ strategic plan to broaden its services portfolio and strengthen its market position in the industry.

news.headline: Kalmar Corp announces share buyback program on February 12, 2024.
Gantry acquires Westcap with $3.2B Loan Portfolio, expanding its Southern California team.
news.summary: A prominent commercial mortgage banking firm, Gantry, based in San Francisco, has recently acquired Westcap along with its loan producers and an impressive loan portfolio valued at $3.2 billion. Gantry, known as the top independent firm in the nation, now boasts a significant increase in assets and capabilities with this strategic move.

news.headline: Topgolf to open massive Burlingame venue with 108 hitting bays on Dec. 13, 2024. 107th global location.
news.summary: Opening on December 13 in Burlingame, California, the three-level venue by Topgolf located along Bayshore Freeway and Airport Drive will feature 108 hitting bays. This marks the addition of the 107th global location for the modern golf entertainment leader.

news.headline: Trust Stamp (Nasdaq: IDAI) announces upcoming events; strategic update and Annual General Meeting details.
news.summary: Atlanta, Georgia - Trust Stamp, a leading global provider of AI-driven technologies, revealed plans for two upcoming events. The announcement was made on December 2, 2024. Trust Stamp is listed on the Nasdaq exchange under the ticker symbol IDAI.

Nelson-Rudie announces Matthew Elhadad as new president of engineering firm; specializes in structural, mechanical, electrical solutions; 48 years old.
news.summary: Minneapolis-based firm Nelson-Rudie & Associates, Inc. has appointed Matt Elhadad as its new president and board chair. With 48 years of experience, the consulting engineering company focuses on structural, mechanical, electrical, and refrigeration solutions for projects across the US and Canada. Elhadad, previously serving as the controller and Board of Directors secretary, played a crucial role in overseeing finance, human resources, and ESOP functions. His strategic contributions have been vital in the establishment and growth of the company.

news.headline: XRP climbs 45%, outstrips Bitcoin and Dogecoin gains in recent surge.
news.summary: Over the past month, XRP, associated with Ripple, has surged in value, surpassing top cryptocurrencies such as Bitcoin and Dogecoin by a significant margin.

news.headline: NASA will cover SpaceX's 31st resupply mission as Dragon spacecraft leaves ISS on Dec 5, 2024, carrying research samples and hardware.
news.summary: On December 2, 2024, NASA and its global collaborators are preparing to receive scientific research samples and equipment from the International Space Station as a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft will leave the ISS on December 5 to journey back to Earth. NASA will broadcast the undocking process live for public viewing.

news.headline: COON (Maine Coon Cat) to be listed on XT Exchange with COON/USDT trading pair in Innovation Zone (MEMEs and WEB3.0), starting Dec 02, 2024.
news.summary: Victoria Mahe, Seychelles - On December 2, 2024, it was revealed that COON, the Maine Coon Cat, is set to be included on XT Exchange. Traders will have access to the COON/USDT trading pair in the Innovation Zone dedicated to MEMEs and WEB3.0. The official launch date is highly anticipated and users are urged to mark their calendars for this event.

news.headline: FAT Brands Foundation launches holiday campaign, matching donations up to $50,000 for charity.
news.summary: FAT Brands has announced its commitment to backing the charitable arm's campaign with a dollar-for-dollar donation match. The company will match contributions up to $50,000, effectively doubling the impact of donations made to the initiative. This move showcases FAT Brands' dedication to supporting philanthropic efforts and making a meaningful difference in the community.

news.headline: Bitcoin's transition to institutional dominance seen as a concern; U.S. ETFs hold 1M tokens (5% of supply). Risk of centralized control noted in government and financial institution influence. Senator proposes buying 1M BTC with Federal Reserve gold. Trump's support possibility heightens worry among purists.
news.summary: The article discusses Bitcoin's shift from a decentralized digital asset to one increasingly controlled by financial institutions and governments. U.S. Bitcoin ETFs now hold over 1 million tokens, equivalent to 5% of its total supply, indicating the establishment's growing influence. Wyoming Senator Cynthia Lummis proposes selling Federal Reserve gold to purchase 1 million Bitcoin, potentially doubling the government's holdings acquired through asset seizures. President-elect Donald Trump's pro-crypto stance raises expectations for such a move. Risk Dimensions founder Mark Connors warns of the risks of centralization by entities like G-10 countries and BlackRock. The article highlights concerns about Bitcoin losing its original decentralized ethos.

news.headline: Canadian cannabis producer Red White & Bloom (CSE:RWB) narrows Q3 net loss as revenue stays flat YoY at CA$21.7 million. EBITDA up 20x YoY to $7.5 million. Company focuses on transitioning to profitable enterprise.
news.summary: Red White & Bloom Brands Inc. (CSE:RWB) released its condensed interim consolidated financial statements on Friday for the third quarter ending on September 30, 2024. The Canadian cannabis company recorded quarterly revenue of CA$21.7 million ($15.47 million), up from CA$20.1 million in the same period the previous year. President Colby De Zen emphasized the company's focus on optimizing the business by evaluating legacy assets and non-core operations. The company saw significant progress in the fiscal year's first nine months, with EBITDA increasing 20 times to approximately $7.5 million and adjusted EBITDA reaching $9.6 million. These improvements highlight the RWB team's efforts to create more profitable revenue streams through organizational efficiencies.

news.headline: Red Light Holland Q2 revenue up 39.1% YoY to CA$1.37 million ($998,263), showing strong financial discipline and reduced net loss.
news.summary: Red Light Holland Corp. reported its financial results for the three months ending Sept. 30, 2024, with revenue of CA$1.37 million, a 39.1% increase from the same period last year. For the six months ended Sept. 30, 2024, revenue was CA$2.93 million, up 33.9% from the same period in 2023. The Ontario-based company produces and sells functional mushrooms, mushroom grow kits, and psilocybin truffles in certain markets. CEO Todd Shapiro noted the company's commitment to growth and financial discipline, emphasizing a 33.9% year-over-year revenue growth. The full story can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Box will release Q3 earnings, with analysts predicting earnings of 42 cents per share and revenue of $275.1 million. Box shares closed at $35.09 on Friday.
news.summary: Box, Inc. will unveil its Q3 earnings after market close on Tuesday, Dec. 3. Analysts predict earnings of 42 cents per share, up from 36 cents in the prior year. Revenue is expected to be $275.1 million for the recent quarter, compared to $261.54 million last year. In a recent move, Box priced an offering of $400 million of convertible senior notes on Sept. 18. Box's stock closed at $35.09, up 0.1% on Friday. For more analyst ratings, visit Benzinga's Analyst Stock Ratings page.

news.headline: US military facing supply crisis as armament stockpiles deplete, worsened by Ukraine war. Dec. 2, 2024 - Pentagon warned.
news.summary: FN Media Group is reporting that the Pentagon is facing a significant issue as its stockpiles of vital armaments are rapidly diminishing due to the extended conflict in Ukraine. The situation has become critical as the war continues to drain resources, with poorly equipped allies facing even greater challenges. This development demands immediate attention as key armaments are being depleted at an alarming rate, potentially impacting military capabilities in the conflict.

news.headline: Diageo, MADD, NFL, and Uber launch "Take a Minute. Make a Plan." campaign to combat impaired driving.
news.summary: Announced in December 2024 during National Impaired Driving Prevention Month, a fresh initiative has been launched to promote responsible behavior among football enthusiasts. Spearheaded by premium beverage brand Diageo North America and Mothers Against Drunk Driving (MADD), the campaign urges fans to avoid driving under the influence. This important effort is crucial in reducing the number of alcohol-related accidents and fatalities, emphasizing the need to plan safe transportation methods.

news.headline: Mindray expands radiology portfolio with Resona-Series ultrasound machines. Sets new standard in digital radiography. Mahwah, NJ, Dec 2, 2024.
news.summary: Mindray, a prominent healthcare technology developer, has introduced the innovative Resona-Series Ultrasound Machines, marking a significant milestone in the industry. The company, based in Mahwah, N.J., announced its plan to expand into the digital radiography sector. The development was revealed on Dec. 2, 2024. Mindray focuses on patient monitoring and anesthesia solutions, setting a new standard with its cutting-edge ultrasound machines. This expansion signifies a strategic move by Mindray to broaden its capabilities and reach in the healthcare technology market.

news.headline: Hilton USA Trust 2016-SFP CMBS holders face uncertainty with special servicer change to Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.
news.summary: A notification has been issued in San Francisco regarding the Hilton USA Trust 2016-SFP commercial mortgage pass-through certificates. Holders are advised of an upcoming transaction concerning the loan’s special servicer, Wells Fargo Bank, N.A. The loan tied to the certificates entered special servicing in June 2023 after Park Hotels & Resorts declared it would cease interest payments and not repay the loan upon maturity. A receiver has been designated to oversee the collateral, which involves the Hilton San property.

news.headline: First Lady Jill Biden, business leaders, and experts converge for the Milken Institute 2024 MEA Summit in Abu Dhabi with global policymakers to address world issues.
news.summary: A summit organized by the Milken Institute in Washington will take place on December 5 and 6, 2024, focusing on the Middle East and Africa (MEA) region. The event will bring together hundreds of prominent figures including business leaders, philanthropists, health and finance experts, and policymakers. The theme of the summit is "Investing in a Shared Future." Attendees, both in-person and virtual, will participate in discussions aimed at addressing global challenges. The summit will be held at the St. Regis Saadiyat Island.

news.headline: Study in JAMA shows weight loss drugs like Wegovy linked to less alcohol use. 14,053 participants, different medications analyzed. Biden suggests Medicare access to weight-loss drugs.
news.summary: A recent study published in JAMA Network Open revealed that the use of anti-obesity medications, such as glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonists, was linked to a reduced risk of both initial onset and recurrence of alcohol use disorder. The research examined data from 14,053 individuals who began using anti-obesity drugs between January 2022 and August 2023, and then refilled the prescription in October or November 2023. The medications were classified into three groups: metformin, bupropion, and naltrexone; first-generation GLP-1 receptor agonists like Victoza (liraglutide) and Trulicity (dulaglutide) from Novo Nordisk A/S and Eli Lilly And Co.; and second-generation GLP-1 receptor agonists. This study sheds light on the potential benefits of these medications in managing alcohol use disorder. In related news, President Biden has proposed granting Medicare and Medicaid access to popular weight-loss drugs. Full details can be found on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Kion highlights FinOps and CloudOps integration for cost-effective cloud operations at AWS re:Invent 2024.
news.summary: At AWS re:Invent 2024, Kion has unveiled a demonstration of integrating FinOps and CloudOps, highlighting its ability to enhance cloud operations in Las Vegas. The showcase emphasizes improved efficiency, security, and cost-effectiveness.

news.headline: Global on-highway vehicle market to grow from 6.6M units in 2023 to 8.9M units by 2030 at 4.4% CAGR due to rising demand for fuel-efficient, low-emission vehicles.
news.summary: A new report on the global On-Highway Vehicle market has been released by ResearchAndMarkets.com. The market was estimated to be at 6.6 million units in 2023 and is expected to increase to 8.9 million units by 2030, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 4.4% during the forecast period. This comprehensive analysis offers insights into market trends, drivers, and projections, assisting in making strategic business decisions. The growth in the on-highway vehicle market is evident from the projected increase in units over the next decade.

news.headline: Anheuser-Busch invests $10M in Jacksonville Brewery, part of $2B spent in last 5 yrs. Driving local economic growth.
news.summary: Anheuser-Busch, a prominent domestic manufacturer and brewer known for brands like Michelob ULTRA and Busch Light, has announced its commitment to ongoing investments in communities. The company has already spent $2 billion over the past 5 years towards this initiative. This news was shared from Jacksonville, Florida on December 2, 2024. Anheuser-Busch, listed on NYSE under the symbol BUD, continues to prioritize community development through its substantial financial contributions.

news.headline: HOW Agency, a subsidiary of 5WPR, wins Gold MarCom Award for Aunt Jackie's Integrated Marketing.
news.summary: On December 2, 2024, The HOW Agency, a digital agency under 5WPR, revealed that their client campaign for Aunt Jackie's secured a Gold award in the Integrated Marketing division at the recent MarCom Awards. This achievement highlights the exceptional work done for Aunt Jackie's by the agency.

news.headline: MongoDB expands AI Applications Program with 6 new partners to enhance AI technology solutions for organizations.
news.summary: Several industry leaders, including Capgemini, Confluent, IBM, QuantumBlack, AI by McKinsey, and Unstructured, have recently become part of the growing ecosystem of the MongoDB AI Applications Program (MAAP). This program aims to assist organizations in leveraging AI to drive impactful outcomes. Additionally, MongoDB and Meta have joined forces to provide support for developers through Meta models and the comprehensive MAAP technology stack. The collaboration between these companies signifies a concerted effort to empower businesses with cutting-edge AI solutions.

news.headline: Kroger's upcoming 12 Merry Days Savings Event starting Dec 4 allows daily deals, redeemable up to 5 times each.
news.summary: CINCINNATI, Dec. 2, 2024 -- The Kroger Co. (NYSE: KR) is set to begin its 12 Merry Days of Deals event on December 4, offering customers the opportunity to redeem a fresh digital deal daily through December 15. Participants can make use of up to five redemptions per day during this promotion.
